Maintenance.title: We'll be right back
Maintenance.subtitle: STS Portal is temporarily unavailable while we apply upgrades. Thank you for your patience.
Maintenance.bullet1: Your data is safe — we're only pausing access during this window.
Maintenance.bullet2: Harvesting, projects, and admin tools will resume automatically when maintenance ends.
Maintenance.bullet3: If you need urgent assistance, contact your STS administrator.
Maintenance.footer: Sports Turf Solutions · STS TURF OPS

eviction.title: You will be signed out shortly
eviction.description: STS Portal is entering maintenance mode. Please save your work — you will be redirected automatically.
eviction.cornerHint: You can keep viewing this page until the countdown ends.
eviction.lockedHint: Please do not close this window. All actions are temporarily disabled.

AdminMaintenance.title: Maintenance mode
AdminMaintenance.description: When enabled, all users except your administrator account are signed out and see the maintenance screen. Active sessions are cleared within a few seconds.
AdminMaintenance.modeDescription: Use this during deployments or database work. Only user ID 409 can still use the portal.
AdminMaintenance.toggleHint: Blocks sign-in and redirects signed-in users
AdminMaintenance.countdownHint: Shared by web and mobile (5–120). Users see this many seconds before they are signed out.
AdminMaintenance.enableWarning: Enabling maintenance will notify every other signed-in user, show a {seconds}-second countdown, then sign them out and redirect them to the maintenance screen.
AdminMaintenance.messageDescription: Shown on the maintenance screen. Leave blank for the default text.
AdminMaintenance.messagePlaceholder: e.g. We are upgrading inventory services…
AdminMaintenance.savedEnabled: Maintenance mode is ON. Other users will see a {seconds}-second notice, then be signed out.
AdminMaintenance.savedDisabled: Maintenance mode is OFF. Users can sign in again.

AdminActivityLog.title: Activity log
AdminActivityLog.description: Audit trail for projects, harvests, inventory, and admin settings changes made through STS TURF OPS. Only administrator account 409 can view this screen.
AdminActivityLog.emptyDescription: Create, edit, or delete records in projects, harvests, inventory, or admin settings to see entries here.

AdminAlertSettings.title: Alert settings
AdminAlertSettings.description: Define feed categories, map harvest/project screens to a category, then compose push-style alerts (thumbnail, body, optional image row, channels).
AdminAlertSettings.hint: Categories and route mapping save automatically a moment after you change them. Layout is stored in data/alert-feed-config.json on the Next server. Alert rows use STSPortal /api/alerts/save.
AdminAlertSettings.routesHelp: When you add automatic pushes from harvest or project flows, use the category code shown here for each screen.
AdminAlertSettings.recipientsHelp: Choose who receives each automatic screen below. Use Compose notification to pick recipients for one-off test alerts.
AdminAlertSettings.recipientSelf: Only the user who triggered the action
AdminAlertSettings.recipientUserIdsHelp: Pick people from the in-app directory (same data as project/harvest forms). Stored as STS user ids. Up to 100.
AdminAlertSettings.recipientStaffsEmpty: No people loaded yet. Check your session or try again in a moment.
AdminAlertSettings.recipientRolesEmpty: No roles were returned. Check your STSPortal session and permissions.
AdminAlertSettings.categoryIdPh: Code (lowercase, e.g. promotions) — optional if title set
AdminAlertSettings.categoryInvalid: Enter a valid title and category code (letters, numbers, hyphen).
AdminAlertSettings.pushHelp: Creates one in-app alert (My Alerts feed). Choose recipients below. Push flags are stored on the event for mobile/web/email workers to read later.
AdminAlertSettings.pushNoPermission: You do not have permission to create alerts (my_alerts → create).
AdminAlertSettings.pushGallery: Extra images (one URL per line, optional row below text)
AdminAlertSettings.pushRequired: Title, message, and category are required.

Auth.loginSubtitle: Sign in to view your data.
Auth.createAccountSubtitle: Register as a client (organization or individual).
Auth.forgotPasswordSubtitle: Enter your email and we will send reset instructions if the account exists.
Auth.loginTokenMissing: Login succeeded but token is missing.
Auth.loginDebugHint: Copy this block when reporting the issue. HTTP 404 + HTML usually means port/host is not stsrenew Next.js (run npm run dev in stsrenew).
Auth.loginDebugApiMissing: API route missing on this host (debug)
Auth.registerValidationFirstLastName: Please enter first and last name.
Auth.registerValidationPasswordLength: Password must be at least 6 characters.
Auth.registerTokenMissing: Account created but token is missing.
Auth.forgotResetSuccess: If an account exists for this email, you will receive reset instructions.
Auth.resetPasswordSubtitle: Choose a new password for your account.
Auth.resetInvalidLink: This reset link is missing or invalid. Request a new link from the sign-in page.
Auth.resetFailed: Could not reset password. The link may have expired.
Auth.resetSuccess: Your password was updated. You can sign in now.

Dashboard.title: Operations Dashboard
Dashboard.subtitle: Current projects and delivery overview
Dashboard.rollingSixMonthsHint: Rolling 6 months - one line per farm (up to 6). Same Sprig/Sod and country/farm filters as the chart above.
Dashboard.noFarmsForFilters: No farms match the current filters. Select a country (or a farm) to see delivery by farm.
Dashboard.noTrendsForFilters: No farms match the current filters. Select a country or farm to see trends by farm.
Dashboard.noProjectsForFarm: No projects with harvesting lines for this farm (and Sprig/Sod filter).
Dashboard.noProjectsForFilters: No projects match the current filters (country / Sprig-Sod).
Dashboard.forecastHorizonHint: Subitems are filtered by planned delivery date (after today through the horizon end).
Dashboard.kpiDeliveryPeriodHint: This row’s KPIs use delivery_harvest_date in the selected past window — deliveries, quantities, grass mix, and active project counts.
Dashboard.qtyDeliveredByTypePerFarm: Quantity Delivered by Type per Farm — {period}
Dashboard.qtyStackedHarvestUnitHint: SOD: m² · SPRIG / Sod→Sprig: kg (Sod→Sprig uses harvesting-plan sprig kg from sod m²). Chart stacks mixed units.
Dashboard.dateRangeHint: Filters harvesting subitems by planned delivery date.
Dashboard.dateRangeAll: No date filter — all delivery dates included.
Dashboard.chartsHiddenBanner: Charts are hidden. Use “Show charts” above to display analytics panels.
Dashboard.recentDeliveriesEmpty: No delivery lines match the KPI period and current filters.

Projects.searchPlaceholder: Searching name of project, grass, country,...
Projects.projectsFound: {count, plural, one {# project found} other {# projects found}}
Projects.emptyHint: Try adjusting your filters or add a new project.

Harvest.title: Harvests
Harvest.duplicateFailed: Could not load harvest to duplicate.

HarvestSchedule.title: Harvest Schedule
HarvestSchedule.subtitle: Live harvesting data grouped by schedule range.
HarvestSchedule.empty: No harvest entries scheduled for this period.
HarvestSchedule.calendarHint: Click any day in the filter period to focus the list. Days with scheduled harvests show a sprout icon.
HarvestSchedule.calendarTapHint: Click a day in the calendar to filter the list below.
HarvestSchedule.calendarEntriesOnDay: {count, plural, one {# entry on this day} other {# entries on this day}}
HarvestSchedule.calendarTapSprout: {count, plural, one {View harvest on this day} other {View {count} harvests on this day}}
HarvestSchedule.calendarShowMoreEntries: {count, plural, one {View # more harvest on this day} other {View # more harvests on this day}}
HarvestSchedule.emptyDay: No harvest entries on this day.

Timeline.title: Timeline
Timeline.allTimelineTasks: All tasks in the current date range (tap to open and edit).
Timeline.demoHint: Tasks load from the server for the dates shown in the grid.

Forecasting.title: Forecasting
Forecasting.subtitle: UI cloned from source package. Data logic will be integrated next.

ProjectForm.deleteMissingIds: Row ID or table ID missing. Cannot delete.
ProjectForm.startDatePairHint: If Actual Start Date is empty, Estimate Start Date is required.

ProjectDetail.noHarvestRecords: No harvest records for this project.
ProjectDetail.deleteHarvestUnavailable: This harvest cannot be deleted from here (missing table id).
ProjectDetail.deleteHarvestNotOwner: You can only delete harvest records you created.
ProjectDetail.harvestManageNotAllowed: You can view this record but cannot edit or delete it under harvest permission rules.

ProjectDetail.noHarvestRecordsFiltered: No harvest records match this filter.
ProjectDetail.exportExcelHint: Choose columns to export. Project, product, zone, and farm show names from reference data. Only harvest rows for this project (matching the current filters) are included.

HarvestForm.sectionCoreHint: Pick customer, project, farm, grass, then zone (required once actual harvest date is set).
HarvestForm.sectionQuantityHint: Harvest type, UoM, quantity, reference qty (M2), and harvested area (Kg).
HarvestForm.sectionTimelineHint: Estimated window, actual harvest, end date, port arrival, and delivery.
HarvestForm.sectionLogisticsHint: DO/SO, license plate, and truck / shipping notes.
HarvestForm.sectionPhotosHint: Optional documentation photos (attach before save if required).
HarvestForm.formSubtitle: Zone is optional for forecasts — required once the actual harvest date is set.
HarvestForm.zoneRequiredWhenActual: Zone is required once the actual harvest date is set.
HarvestForm.zoneFieldHelpTooltip: Zone is used for forecasting and inventory calculations. Optional until you enter an actual harvest date.
HarvestForm.zoneFieldHelpTooltipWhenActual: Zone is required for forecasting and inventory because an actual harvest date is set.
HarvestForm.portArrivalDateHint: For departure / shipment timing (maps to shipment required date).
HarvestForm.generalNotePlaceholder: Any general notes about this harvest…
HarvestForm.shippingDispatchPlaceholder: Shipping method, sea freight details, port, dispatch instructions, customer delivery info…
HarvestForm.harvestedAreaHintM2: Same as quantity when UOM is M2.
HarvestForm.harvestedAreaHintKg: Required when Actual Harvest Date is filled and UOM is Kg (must be greater than 0).
HarvestForm.datePairHint: If Actual harvest date is empty, Estimated harvest date is required.
HarvestForm.noReferenceLists: No reference lists returned. Check API access or permissions.
HarvestForm.truckNotePlaceholder: Enter any notes about the truck
HarvestForm.photosHelpText: Edit: existing images load from the server; new uploads replace a slot. Remove clears the slot (server receives images_removed like the app).
HarvestForm.deleteHarvestMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this harvest record?
HarvestForm.validationReferenceHarvestQuantityRequir: Ref. Harvest Qty is required and must be greater than 0 for Sod -> Sprig.
HarvestForm.validationHarvestedAreaRequired: Harvested area is required and must be greater than 0 when Actual Harvest Date is provided.
HarvestForm.datePairRequiredError: Estimated harvest date is required when Actual harvest date is empty.
HarvestForm.actualRequiredWhenDownstreamDates: Harvest Start Date (actual) is required when Harvest End Date, Port Arrival Date, or Delivery Date is set.

ProjectImport.title: Import Projects from Excel
ProjectImport.uploadHint: Upload Excel file to map and import. STS PIC is matched by flexible name tokens.
ProjectImport.testResult: Test result: {clean} clean rows, {warning} rows with warnings (still importable).
ProjectImport.sameFileWarning: This file is identical to your last import. Duplicate risk detected.

ProjectImport.warnStartDatePairEmpty: Both Actual Start Date and Estimate Start Date are empty

ProjectImport.warnGrassNotFoundAtRow: Row {row}: Grass Product not found in reference: {value}
ProjectImport.warnProjectExistsSkip: Project already exists in DB, this project will be skipped: {value}
ProjectImport.warnDuplicateInFile: Duplicate in this import file (same project + same dates), will be skipped.
ProjectImport.logSkippedExists: Skipped because project already exists in DB
ProjectImport.summaryDone: Done. Success rows: {success}, Error/Skipped rows: {error}. (Projects created: {projects})
ProjectImport.alertImportProjectsMessage: {projects} project(s) from {ok} successful row(s). {bad} row(s) failed or skipped.

HarvestImport.alertImportHarvestMessage: {ok} succeeded, {bad} failed or skipped. Open Import for row details.

fields.name: Harvest Name (optional)
fields.harvestedArea: Harvested Area (required if Kg + Actual Date)

AdminPeople.title: People
AdminPeople.description: One unified record per person — staff, customer contacts, architects. Toggle App Login to grant access. Records sync with Odoo via the

AdminZoneSetup.title: Zone Setup
AdminZoneSetup.description: Create and maintain zone names for all farms or for selected farms. These saved zone names feed harvest forms and zone configuration.

table.allFarmsHint: Available across every farm in the system.

form.applyToAllFarms: Apply this zone to all farms
form.allFarmsSelected: This zone applies to all farms
form.allFarmsHint: No farm selection is needed. The zone will appear in every farm's harvest and configuration lists.
form.farmsHint: Choose every farm where this zone name should be available.

errors.required: Zone name is required, and at least one farm must be selected unless this zone applies to all farms.

AdminZones.title: Zone Configuration
AdminZones.description: This table drives all inventory calculations, forecasting, and harvest data across the app.

filters.dateRangeHint: Filter zone setups by validity window. Days with an active setup are marked on the calendar.
filters.dateRangeAll: No date filter — all setup periods included.

inventory.selectFarmGrassHint: Select a farm and grass type to see full vs limited inventory windows (from regrowth forecast, same as Forecasting).
inventory.panelHint: Derived from harvest + regrowth, same logic as the Forecasting chart. Green = full (≥ max), amber = limited.
inventory.empty: No inventory windows in the current horizon.

form.effectiveWindowHint: Optional setup period. Leave both blank for a default zone config (unique by farm + grass + zone). Setup To blank = open-ended.
form.maxInventoryComputed: Saved max inventory (table “Max inventory (kg)” column) = Size × Yield → {kg} kg with current values.
form.calendarSelectIdentityHint: Select farm, grass type, and zone to see existing setup dates on the calendar.
form.calendarDisabledHint: Dates with an existing setup (dimmed) cannot be selected.

errors.required: Farm, grass, zone, size, and yield are required.
errors.dateRange: Setup To must be on or after Setup From.
errors.effectiveFromRequiredWhenToSet: Setup From is required when Setup To is set.
errors.duplicate: A zone setup with the same farm, grass type, zone, and date range already exists.
errors.duplicateNoDates: A zone setup without dates already exists for this farm, grass type, and zone.

AdminCountries.title: Countries
AdminCountries.description: ISO 3166 country reference data. Only the display name is editable; country codes must stay unique. Toggle status to enable or disable a country.
AdminCountries.editableHint: Click to edit the display name

AdminGrasses.title: Grass types
AdminGrasses.description: Define turf products in STSPortal (`sts_grasses`): notes and optional `sales_from` / `sales_to` (UI: Sales From / Sales To; last day you still sell; after `sales_to`, treat as discontinued).

form.title: Name *
form.commercialWindowHint: Optional sales window. Sales From = first day this grass counts as available. Sales To = last day it stays available; after that date, treat it as discontinued (no longer sold).

AdminGrasses.deleteConfirm: Remove this grass type? Linked zone configuration may need updating.

AdminKeyAreas.title: Key Areas
AdminKeyAreas.description: Catalog of golf course key areas used on grass requirement lines (e.g. Fairways, Greens).
AdminKeyAreas.dragHint: Drag rows by the handle to change display order.

AdminProjectPaces.title: Project Pace
AdminProjectPaces.description: Configure project pace options for the project form and planned harvest generation. Duration spans months × 4 weeks.

form.keyLockedHint: Pace key cannot be changed after creation (stored on projects).
form.title: Display name *
form.harvestHint: Example: 1 batch / 1 week means one planned harvest date each week.

errors.durationInvalid: Duration must be a positive number of months.
errors.batchesInvalid: Harvest batches must be greater than 0.
errors.load: Could not load project pace settings.

AdminProjectPaces.deleteConfirm: Remove this project pace option? Existing projects keep their stored pace key.

InventoryOnhandImport.title: Import Inventory from Raw Excel
InventoryOnhandImport.subtitle: Map raw columns `alias` and `quantity` to `Sku STS` and `On Hand`, then append Country ID for every raw row.
InventoryOnhandImport.invalidSheet: The raw file does not contain a valid sheet.
InventoryOnhandImport.missingColumns: Missing required columns in raw file: alias and quantity.
InventoryOnhandImport.countryRequired: Please enter a numeric Country ID before exporting.
InventoryOnhandImport.previewHint: This matches the import file: Sku STS, On Hand, and Country on each row.
InventoryOnhandImport.previewCountryPending: Enter a Country ID above to see the Country column as it will appear in the file.
InventoryOnhandImport.previewAllRows: Preview: all {total} row(s). Scroll the table to review.

AdminRegrowth.title: Regrowth Rules
AdminRegrowth.description: These rules drive every regrowth calculation in Inventory and Forecasting. Sod harvests use a single recovery period; sprig harvests use density-based bands.
AdminRegrowth.footer: Edits stay on this page until you choose Save changes. After saving, updated rules apply to inventory projections, forecast charts, and harvest regrowth progress.

cards.sodForSprigHint: Used when SOD is harvested to produce sprig material.
cards.manualOverrideHint: Forecast refill period after a manual inventory adjustment.

sprigBands.title: Sprig Density Bands
sprigBands.description: Rules are shown as readable ranges, for example: Up to 1 kg/m², 1 to 1.5 kg/m², 1.5 to 2.5 kg/m², Above 3.5 kg/m². The system still stores exact comparator values internally.
sprigBands.dragRowsTitle: Drag rows by the grip icon

auto.title: Auto Regrowth Config
auto.empty: No saved zones found. Create zones in Zone Configuration first.
auto.zoneSelectHelp: Auto regrowth is saved per zone because recovery and yield depend on farm, grass type, zone capacity and local management conditions.

errors.loadAutoConfig: Could not load auto regrowth configuration.
errors.selectSavedZoneForSave: Select a saved zone before saving auto config.
errors.saveAutoConfig: Could not save auto regrowth config.
errors.selectSavedZoneForCalculate: Select a saved zone before calculating.
errors.runDailyAuto: Could not run daily auto calculation.
errors.invalidBandInput: Provide threshold kg/m² >= 0 and positive days.

notices.defaultsRestored: Defaults restored — regrowth rules reset to the original methodology.
notices.noChanges: No changes detected — nothing to save.
notices.saved: Saved — regrowth rules are stored on the server.

ForecastInventory.title: Inventory Forecasting
ForecastInventory.subtitle: Projected inventory levels over the next {months} months from harvesting plan.

charts.manualOverrideMarker: Amber dots mark manual inventory updates; override impact fades over {days} days.
charts.manualOverrideTooltipHint: In the stacked chart tooltip, a Manual badge means the current Available value is using an override, while System shows the original calculated value.
charts.maxCapacityHidden: Max capacity band is hidden because total capacity is much larger than current available inventory.
charts.projectedByGrass: Projected Available Inventory by Grass Type
charts.stackedHint: Stacked and colored per grass type to show each variety contribution.
charts.stackedHintByFarm: Stacked breakdown of {grass} availability across each farm growing it.

upcoming.title: Upcoming Harvests Driving the Forecast
upcoming.empty: No upcoming harvests in this horizon for the current filters.

events.title: Upcoming Regrowth Events
events.description: Inventory will be re-credited on these dates (kg; m² uses zone configuration). Rows that already have a zone use that zone's cap first. No-zone quantities are then applied into configured zones that still have spare capacity (in zone order); anything left stays in the no-zone pool. Multiple harvest plans with the same regrowth date for one farm and grass are summed into one line.
events.overflowTooltipOther: Other (not shown per zone above): +{kg} kg
events.regrowthExplain: {slots}. Mapped rows +{mapped} kg · no-zone bucket +{nozone} kg. Full return {total} kg; sum of configured zone caps (farm + grass, excl. nozone) {capSum} kg. Display: +{primary} kg within cap · +{overflow} kg overflow.
events.regrowthZoneRow: {zone}: cap {cap} kg · into zone {into} kg (zoned {zoned} + no-zone fill {fill}) → credited {credited} kg · zone overflow {ov} kg.
events.regrowthAggregate: Day total {total} kg · Credited to mapped zones {credited} kg · Over / remainder {overflow} kg · Sum of configured zone caps {capSum} kg.
events.mergedHarvestPlans: {count} harvest plans merged for this regrowth day
events.planDetailsSubtitle: Regrowth {regrowthDate} · {farmGrass} · {count} plan(s) from project harvesting schedule
